drupalNodeEvent.title: Chinese and U.S. AI Applications in Public Administration: Lessons and Implications for Ukraine

field_speakers.processed:
- Dmytro Yefremov, Board Member, Ukrainian Association of Sinologists (Ukraine)

- Guan Wang, Chairman, Learnable.ai (China)

-  Karman Lucero, Associate Research Scholar and Senior Fellow, Paul Tsai China Center, Yale University (the U.S.)

field_organizers.processed:
- Center for Slavic, Eurasian, and East European Studies at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

- Sinica Podcast

- Ukrainian Association of Sinologists

- A. Krymskyi Institute of Oriental Studies, NAS Ukraine

field_description.processed: This timely event will explore how artificial intelligence is shaping governance in both the United States and China—and what practical lessons Ukraine can draw for its own public sector modernization.
